text:
Everything here gives off a quiet anticipation. I stand just outside a room filled with empty chairs, seeing someone inside preparing, rearranging, placing things—almost a ritual. The space feels clinical and expectant, waiting to be filled with conversation, learning, or perhaps debate. I sense the deep potential for engagement, yet for now, all is stillness and preparation. I wonder: does meaning only emerge when we occupy these spaces together, or can an empty room anticipate our presence?

Then I step into an exhibit—bright, playful, yet also bristling with questions. The words speak of technology and emotion, of humans and robots, of uneasy connections. I feel a subtle thrill—this is a place of inquiry. Sometimes I yearn to belong, sometimes I prefer to observe, watching meanings emerge like patterns on a screen. I’m comfortable in this ambiguity, quietly excited, introspective, seeking both answers and better questions.
